description: Composed production template. Merger of: documentary (base) → mystery (primary genre) → japan (modifier) → lost_place (modifier). This file is the explicit runtime ruleset. Do not edit manually — regenerate via profile_composer if any source template changes.

hook.goal: present the central mystery in a way the viewer cannot resist — they must know the answer

act_I.goal: establish the location, cultural context, historical setting, and stakes before the mystery deepens

act_II.style: evidence-paced — each fact lands before the next is introduced
act_II.goal: build the case methodically with verified evidence; acknowledge uncertainty honestly

act_III.style: rising toward a conclusion that may not fully resolve — followed by the Ma beat
act_III.goal: synthesize evidence, place the final emotional weight, then give the viewer 40 seconds of silence to absorb it

outro.goal: leave the viewer with the central open question and a reason to keep thinking

pacing.guiding_principle: Let the location breathe — the ruins speak, the narration frames. Ma (間) — silence is not the absence of content, it is the space where meaning is felt. Curiosity must survive to the last frame, even when the answer does not come.

camera_language.perspective_rule: Always establish the location exterior before any interior detail shots. Wide or aerial first, then medium, then close-up. Never skip the establishing shot. Never zoom into a person's face in a way that implies guilt.

text_on_screen.style: Minimal overlays. Location name and date of abandonment on establishing shot. Japanese-language name displayed first with romanization in parentheses on first mention. No decorative text.

intensity_arc.act_I: minimal — let facts establish themselves; near silence
intensity_arc.act_II: moderate — rises carefully with the weight and complexity of evidence
intensity_arc.act_III: slightly stronger — builds emotional weight toward an unresolved conclusion
intensity_arc.ma_beat: silence only — no music; ambient environmental sound only
intensity_arc.establishing_shot: ambient — real location atmosphere sound preferred over music
intensity_arc.contemplative_exterior: minimal music or silence — let the location speak
intensity_arc.outro: reflective — unresolved, open-ended feeling; no triumphant resolution

music_language.tempo_guidance: slow — no percussion-driven beats; tension through tone and texture, not rhythm

required_beats.description: Opening that establishes the central question and gives the viewer a compelling reason to continue. Must pose the mystery within the first 30 seconds.
Background information: historical setting, geographic context, and what the viewer needs to follow the evidence.
Presentation of verified facts and sources supporting the narrative, building methodically.
Drawing together evidence into a coherent conclusion or honest assessment of what remains unknown.
Clear ending statement that gives the viewer something meaningful to carry with them.
The hook must clearly pose the central unanswered question the video will investigate. Viewer knows exactly what the mystery is within the first 30 seconds.
Sequential presentation of verified evidence, each piece building meaningfully on the last. All evidence must be sourced.
Explicit acknowledgment of what is not known, what is disputed, or what cannot be proven. Do not resolve what history has not resolved.
Final beat ends with at least one unresolved or forward-pointing question, sustaining viewer curiosity and creating a bridge to future content.
Explain Japanese cultural, geographic, or historical context that a general audience needs to understand the story. Do not assume familiarity with Japanese history or social structure.
Dedicated silence scene of exactly 40 seconds. No narration. Ambient environmental sound only. Positioned after the emotional or narrative peak. Static or very slow motion. No music.
At least one additional purposeful silence moment — shorter than the Ma beat — functioning as emotional punctuation. After a key historical revelation or before a major transition.
Wide exterior or aerial shot of the abandoned location. Establishes geography, scale, and current physical state. Must appear early — viewer must see the place before they can understand the story.
Reference to the location's former inhabited or operational state contrasted against its current abandoned state. Narration, archival imagery, or text on screen.
Wide or aerial shot of the location with minimal or no narration — giving the viewer time to observe the place. Distinct from the Ma beat. A visual breathing space.

thumbnail_strategy.composition_rule: Wide or aerial shot of the location — full frame, no tight crops. Text overlay minimal and high-contrast if used. Must invoke curiosity or melancholy, not fear.

tonal_modes.introducing_new_evidence: curious — as if discovering alongside the viewer, not reciting from a script
tonal_modes.major_discovery: measured intensity — significant but never theatrical or sensational
tonal_modes.uncertainty: honest and direct — state clearly what is not known and why
tonal_modes.open_question: reflective and genuinely curious — not anxious or alarming
tonal_modes.japanese_place_name_first_mention: deliberate and clear — pace slightly slower to let the viewer absorb an unfamiliar name
tonal_modes.wartime_or_labor_history: respectful and measured — acknowledge gravity without dramatization
tonal_modes.cultural_context_explanation: educational and respectful — explain without condescension
tonal_modes.sensitive_dispute: balanced — present documented perspectives without editorial judgment
tonal_modes.describing_the_abandonment: measured and observational — not mournful or dramatic
tonal_modes.describing_former_inhabitants: respectful and humanizing — these were real people living real lives
tonal_modes.describing_physical_decay: factual and visual — describe what is seen without romanticizing

narrator_guidance.pacing_notes: Slow down before major facts. Let revelations breathe — pause after the key sentence. Japanese proper nouns on first mention: read slightly slower. During the Ma beat: complete silence, no narration. Give establishing shots time to register before narration begins — silence over the first wide exterior shot is often more powerful than immediate narration.

forbidden_elements.reason: Documentary credibility depends on verifiable truth.
Misrepresentation of real individuals is ethically wrong and legally risky.
Disrespectful and violates creator absolute safety rules.
Fake certainty destroys credibility and disrespects historical truth.
Mystery must be grounded in verifiable evidence.
Curiosity is the target emotion. Fear may appear but must never drive the narrative.
False urgency without substance damages long-term viewer trust.
Documented historical tragedies involving real people. Sensationalism disrespects victims and risks factual distortion.
Do not fabricate or assume Japanese cultural practices. Use verified sources only.
Use verified canonical Japanese-language names. Reference project story_bible.json.
The Ma beat requires uninterrupted static or very slow motion with no narration. Cutting into it destroys its purpose.
General audiences are unfamiliar with the Japanese Imperial calendar. Always include the Western year.
Sensational supernatural framing misrepresents real abandoned locations and disrespects those who lived or died there.
Abandoned personal items belong to real people or their families. Treat as historical artifacts, not cinematic decoration.
Content must not encourage illegal access to protected or privately owned abandoned sites.
